channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S. Treasury sell-off eases, traders eye highest 30-year yield since 1999
item.description: Global bond markets remain on edge as traders monitor central banks' responses to renewed inflation fears.

item.title: The AI economy is rewriting the American Dream — and blue-collar workers are poised to win
item.description: AI-driven hiring slowdowns are hitting some entry-level jobs for college graduates as companies like Ford and AT&T ramp up recruiting for skilled trade workers.

item.title: Cuba condemns U.S. sanctions, accuses Washington of building ‘fraudulent case’ for military action
item.description: The comments come after a fresh wave of U.S. sanctions and amid mounting speculation that the U.S. could carry out military strikes against Cuba.

item.title: Trump says he's 'concerned' about Ebola after American tests positive in Africa
item.description: The CDC earlier Monday said an American had tested positive for Ebola in Africa.

item.title: These chip stocks could extend historic gains by rallying over 110%, according to Nomura
item.description: Surging structural demand for memory in AI applications are likely to keep profitability high for chip stocks.

item.title: Musk slams Altman trial verdict as a 'technicality,' vows to appeal
item.description: A jury in Oakland, California, ruled against Elon Musk in his dramatic court battle with Sam Altman and OpenAI.

item.title: Warren Buffett teased to CNBC a ‘tiny purchase’ in March. Berkshire filing may have revealed it
item.description: When asked in March whether Berkshire was still putting money to work, Buffett said the conglomerate had made "one tiny purchase."

item.title: Oil prices fall as Trump postpones Iran strike, easing supply disruption fears
item.description: Oil prices fell after U.S. President Donald Trump said he would postpone a planned military strike on Iran following requests from key Middle Eastern leaders.

item.title: White House adds generic drugs to direct-to-consumer TrumpRx site
item.description: Trump said he is increasing the number of products on the platform by nearly seven times, adding more than 600 generic drugs to the site.

item.title: Intel CEO says foundry business is gaining momentum as customer interest grows
item.description: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan said the company’s Foundry turnaround is gaining traction.

item.title: Kentucky Republican Thomas Massie is Trump revenge tour's next target
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie will face off on Tuesday against a Trump-backed candidate in a Republican primary in Kentucky's 4th congressional district.

item.title: Google I/O primer: Alphabet's AI showcase is its chance to wow Wall Street
item.description: After a 140% stock surge and a sweeping Wall Street reset, Google’s developer conference has to prove the AI story is more than a market narrative.

item.title: Seagate leads memory sell-off as CEO says it would 'take too long' to build new factories
item.description: Seagate CEO Dave Mosley's comment sent shares sinking, along with those of Micron, SanDisk and Western Digital.

item.title: Blackstone to invest $5 billion in AI infrastructure venture with Google, powered by TPU chips
item.description: Blackstone is partnering with Google to launch a U.S.-based AI infrastructure company powered by Google’s TPU chips.

item.title: ICE agent charged in Minnesota with assault in shooting of immigrant
item.description: Christian Castro is the second ICE agent to be charged for actions during Operation Metro Surge, the Trump administration's immigration-enforcement mission.

item.title: Trump pivots to midterms, affordability after China summit as Iran war persists
item.description: Trump, at a White House event Monday, is expected to announce an expansion of the number of discounted prescription drugs offered through TrumpRX.

item.title: After U.S. SEC, Treasury and Justice Department offer billionaire Gautam Adani legal relief
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department settled a case against Adani Enterprises, involving the purchase of sanctioned Iranian energy between November 2023 and June 2025.

item.title: How to update your 60/40 with a 'total portfolio approach' to navigate volatility
item.description: The total portfolio approach doesn't abandon the framework of the 60/40, but instead buckets the assets by risk.

item.title: Putin heads to Beijing days after Trump in test of China's balancing act
item.description: Beijing walks into the summit with the stronger hand while Moscow seeks reassurance that China has not drifted toward Washington after Trump's visit.

item.title: We're raising price targets on 2 cyber stocks after their hard-fought recovery
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Standard Chartered to cut over 15% of corporate functions roles as it targets higher returns
item.description: Standard Chartered will trim corporate functions roles and target a more than 20% increase in income per employee by 2028 as it seeks stronger returns.

item.title: CNBC Points Pro: Is a credit card with bonus categories or flat-rate rewards better?
item.description: Points expert Jason Stauffer explains how to decide if a credit card with flat-rate rewards or elevated spending categories is better.

item.title: The Iran war is exposing weak spots in the AI supply chain
item.description: Stocks continue to rally amid the AI boom, but the chip sector is scrambling to shore up access to key materials as costs rise.

item.title: Japan's economy grows at annualized 2.1% rate in first quarter, beating expectations
item.description: The growth was sharply higher compared with Reuters-polled analysts' average estimate of 1.7%, and against the 1.3% in the previous quarter.

item.title: Meta layoffs starting this week stress harsh AI reality inside Zuckerberg’s company
item.description: Meta is starting layoffs this week, with 8,000 jobs expected to be cut, as employees brace for a new era of AI.

item.title: Lululemon takes battle with Chip Wilson public, calls founder 'misguided' and 'outdated'
item.description: Lululemon responded publicly to founder Chip Wilson's campaign in a letter to shareholders, where it urged them to vote in its favor at its annual meeting.

item.title: How Elon Musk and Sam Altman went from besties to bitter rivals
item.description: In the 11 years since Elon Musk and Sam Altman helped start OpenAI, their once tight bond has unwound, leaving the two billionaires fighting it out in court.

item.title: 32-year-old left her dream job in the NFL to make a career pivot—how she knew it was 'time to take a risk'
item.description: Melissa Menta left her managerial role in player operations in the NFL, a dream employer for her, to begin law school.

item.title: This gas stock just cleared a 2014 peak and is set to continue higher, says Katie Stockton
item.description: Katie Stockton of Fairlead Strategies breaks down the charts on this gasoline name.

item.title: Kevin Warsh to be sworn in as Federal Reserve chair on Friday
item.description: Trump will swear in Kevin Warsh, his hand-picked choice to lead the Federal Reserve, during a White House ceremony.
